title: The Shiny New Phone
scenario: Your old phone still works, but a new model just dropped. You have some surplus this month. Start an emergency fund or buy the phone?

choices.label: Buy the new phone now
choices.lesson: Upgrading things that already work is 'lifestyle inflation'. It feels small each time but quietly eats money that could protect you later.

choices.label: Start an emergency fund
choices.lesson: An emergency fund of 3-6 months of expenses is your first financial shock absorber. Build it before you chase upgrades.
